behaviors.description: Reject a plugin with a missing required manifest field before any code from that plugin executes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at a module boundary (manifest validation must gate module import/execution), not just pure schema logic. REAL-INFRA: No real HTTP/subprocess/external OS primitive is required beyond local filesystem/module wiring. MOCK-HIDE: A unit test of only `validateManifest` can pass while loader still imports plugin code too early; wiring test is needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: System is rejected because behavior text only requires loader+manifest+import ordering in-process, not subprocess/network/real external API.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with an invalid `manifest.json` and an entry module with a detectable side effect (e.g., writes a marker). Run real loader against that directory and assert: (1) validation error reported, (2) marker absent, (3) `onLoad` not called.

behaviors.description: If plugin A depends on plugin B, call B.onLoad before A.onLoad.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure appears when dependency resolution output is handed to lifecycle executor across modules. REAL-INFRA: In-process plugin graph + local fixtures are sufficient; no subprocess/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: Unit-testing topological sort alone can miss executor ordering bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because ordering is internal orchestration, not OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use two real fixture plugins on disk (`A -> B`) whose `onLoad` hooks append to a shared in-memory or file-backed trace. Run loader and assert trace order is `B` then `A`.

behaviors.description: If two plugins depend on each other, report the cycle and load neither plugin.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Needs interaction between dependency graph detection and load suppression; pure cycle-detector unit test is insufficient for "loads neither". REAL-INFRA: No external system required beyond local plugin wiring. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked executor may hide accidental partial loads after cycle detection. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected; behavior text is about internal dependency resolution and initialization gating only.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins `A` and `B` with mutual dependencies and observable import/onLoad side effects. Execute loader and assert: cycle error includes both plugin names, and neither plugin import side effect nor `onLoad` runs.

behaviors.description: Invoke a plugin’s onLoad only after its entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is explicitly about sequencing across module import execution and lifecycle hook invocation. REAL-INFRA: Requires real in-process module loading semantics, but not subprocess/network/external API. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing import can mask timing/ordering regressions with actual module side effects. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require OS-level behavior beyond normal in-process module execution.
behaviors.test_description: Build a fixture plugin whose entry module sets a flag during top-level execution and whose `onLoad` asserts that flag is already set (or records ordering events). Run loader and assert no ordering violation.

behaviors.description: If a plugin throws during onLoad, continue initializing remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure occurs in cross-module orchestration (error propagation + dependency-aware continuation), not one function alone. REAL-INFRA: Local fixture plugins and loader wiring are sufficient; no real network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Unit tests of error handler or scheduler alone can miss that real runner aborts globally. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because requirement is internal initialization control flow, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugin set where `X` throws in `onLoad`, `Y` is independent, and `Z` depends on `X`. Run loader and assert: `Y` loads successfully, `Z` is skipped/failed due to dependency, and error for `X` is reported without global abort.
